steps.title: Expand the squared term and simplify linear components
steps.content:
Start by expanding $(2x + 5)^2$ using the perfect square pattern:

$$(2x + 5)^2 = (2x)^2 + 2(2x)(5) + 5^2 = 4x^2 + 20x + 25$$

For the remaining terms, apply the signs and coefficients:
$$-(x - 2) = -x + 2$$
$$2(x + 3) = 2x + 6$$

steps.title: Combine all like terms
steps.content:
Assemble all expanded parts:
$$4x^2 + 20x + 25 - x + 2 + 2x + 6$$

Collect by degree:
  • $x^2$ terms: $4x^2$
  • $x$ terms: $20x - x + 2x = 21x$
  • Constant terms: $25 + 2 + 6 = 33$

The equivalent expression is:
$$4x^2 + 21x + 33$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
